questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Coefficient of thermal conductivity is the product of heat, distance and reciprocal of (area <math> <mo>×</mo> </math> difference in temperature <math><mo>×</mo></math> time). The new value of a unit of coefficient of thermal conductivity, if fundamental units are <math><mn>21.6</mn><mtext>kg</mtext></math>, <math> <mrow> <mtext>1 decimetre</mtext></mrow></math>, <math> <mrow> <mtext>4 K</mtext> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <mtext>1 minute </mtext></mrow></math>will be ______ <math><mo>×</mo><msup><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup></math> new units.
questions.solution:
Coefficient of thermal conductivity, k=Q×lA×Δθ×t

⇒ K=ml2t2×ll2×Δθ×t

⇒ K=m×lΔθ×t3=21.6×0.14×603

=2.5×10-6 new units
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The lengths of sides of a cuboid are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>. If the relative percentage error in the measurement of a is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>%</mo></math>, then what is the relative percentage error in the measurement of the volume of the cube.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The density of a sphere is measured by measuring the mass and diameter. If it is known that the maximum percentage errors in the measurement of mass and diameter are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>%</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>%</mo></math> respectively then the maximum percentage error in the measurement of density is
questions.solution:
The density can be computed by 

ρ=mv=mπd36

or ρ ∝ md3

 ∴∆ρρ×100%=∆mm×100% + 3∆dd×100%

or ∆ρρ×100=2+3×3=11

Hence, the percentage error in measurement of density is 11%
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">ε</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub></math> denote the dimensional formula of the permittivity of vacuum. If <math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>=</mo></math> mass, <math><mtext>L</mtext><mo>=</mo></math> length, <math><mtext>T</mtext><mo>=</mo></math> time and <math><mtext>A</mtext><mo>=</mo></math> electric current, then dimensions of permittivity is given as <math> <mrow> <mfenced close="]" open="["> <mrow> <msup> <mtext>M</mtext> <mtext>p</mtext> </msup> <msup> <mtext>L</mtext> <mtext>q</mtext> </msup> <msup> <mtext>T</mtext> <mtext>r</mtext> </msup> <msup> <mtext>A</mtext> <mtext>s</mtext> </msup> </mrow> </mfenced></mrow> </math>. Find the value of <math><mfrac><mrow><mtext>p</mtext><mo>-</mo><mtext>q</mtext><mo>+</mo><mtext>r</mtext></mrow><mrow><mtext>s</mtext></mrow></mfrac></math>
questions.solution:
From Coulomb's law,
F=q1q24πε0r2
∴ε0=q1q24πFr2=(A1T1)(A1T1)[M1L1T-2][L2]=M-1L-3T4A2
∴p=-1,q=-3,r=4,s=2
∴p-q+rs=-1-(-3)+42=62=3
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
